req.title: Audit log access
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-014 mandates that a privacy-officer may read AuditEvent records scoped to their own organisation and must be denied access to records belonging to a different organisation. Three test cases are derived: (1) EP positive – an officer queries their own org's logs and receives 200 with matching records; (2) NEG negative – the same officer queries a different org's logs and receives 403; (3) BVA boundary – the officer queries logs at the exact boundary where the org filter switches from their own org to another (single shared record whose agent org is ambiguous), confirming the server enforces strict ownership and returns only the record attributed to the officer's org.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_logs
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for organisation ORG-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to ORG-A
actions: Authenticate as the ORG-A privacy-officer and obtain a bearer token
expected: Response bundle contains at least one AuditEvent entry
Every returned AuditEvent has agent[].who.reference containing ORG-A
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_logs(
    auth_token_for_org,   # <fixture>: returns bearer token for (role, org)
    api_base_url,         # <fixture>: base URL string
    seed_audit_event,     # <fixture>: creates AuditEvent for org, yields id, cleans up
):
    org = "ORG-A"
    token = auth_token_for_org(role="privacy-officer", org=org)
    seed_audit_event(org=org)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent.organization": org},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    bundle = resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Expected at least one AuditEvent entry"
    for entry in entries:
        resource = entry.get("resource", {})
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
        assert any(org in ref for ref in refs), (
            f"AuditEvent {resource.get('id')} not scoped to {org}: {refs}"
        )

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_logs
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for organisation ORG-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to ORG-B
actions: Authenticate as the ORG-A privacy-officer and obtain a bearer token
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or equivalent error descriptor
trace: R-HLT-014 – cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
Error pathway: 403 when crossing org boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_logs(
    auth_token_for_org,   # <fixture>
    api_base_url,         # <fixture>
    seed_audit_event,     # <fixture>
):
    officer_org = "ORG-A"
    target_org  = "ORG-B"
    token = auth_token_for_org(role="privacy-officer", org=officer_org)
    seed_audit_event(org=target_org)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent.organization": target_org},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 Forbidden for cross-org access, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    # Accept FHIR OperationOutcome or a generic error key
    assert (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in body
        or "issue" in body
    ), f"Expected error descriptor in body: {body}"

tests.name: test_audit_log_boundary_single_record_at_org_edge
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for organisation ORG-A
Exactly one AuditEvent exists scoped to ORG-A (the minimum non-empty result set)
No additional AuditEvents for ORG-A exist beyond this single record
actions: Authenticate as the ORG-A privacy-officer and obtain a bearer token
Confirm exactly one record is returned
Attempt GET /AuditEvent?agent.organization=ORG-B with the same token to confirm 403
expected: First request returns HTTP 200 with a bundle containing exactly one AuditEvent
That single AuditEvent is scoped to ORG-A
Second request returns HTTP 403 (boundary between allowed and forbidden org)
trace: R-HLT-014 – records returned within org (minimum boundary: 1 record)
R-HLT-014 – 403 when crossing org boundary (first record outside scope)
BVA on result-set size lower bound and org-scope switching point
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_audit_log_boundary_single_record_at_org_edge(
    auth_token_for_org,      # <fixture>
    api_base_url,            # <fixture>
    seed_exactly_one_audit_event,  # <fixture>: seeds exactly 1 AuditEvent for org, cleans up
):
    org_a = "ORG-A"
    org_b = "ORG-B"
    token = auth_token_for_org(role="privacy-officer", org=org_a)
    seed_exactly_one_audit_event(org=org_a)

    # Boundary: minimum non-empty in-scope result
    resp_own = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent.organization": org_a},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp_own.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp_own.status_code}"
    entries = resp_own.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 AuditEvent, got {len(entries)}"
    agents = entries[0]["resource"].get("agent", [])
    refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
    assert any(org_a in r for r in refs), f"Record not scoped to {org_a}: {refs}"

    # Boundary: first record outside scope must yield 403
    resp_cross = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent.organization": org_b},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp_cross.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 at org boundary, got {resp_cross.status_code}"
    )
